revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kagoshima_Gokoku_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''護国神社'''（ごこくじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島市]]草牟田にある[[別表神社]]。正式名「'''鹿児島県護國神社'''」。
== 沿革 == [[戊辰戦争]]で戦死した兵士を祭る「''靖献霊社''」に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に作られていた。[[1941年]]（[[昭和16年]]）に現在地に移転、神社名を現名称に変更する。
[[Category:鹿児島県の神社|ここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|かこしまここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かこしまここくしんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: [[護国神社 (鹿児島市)]] を [[鹿児島県護国神社]] へ移動: 正式名称に
revision.text:
'''護国神社'''（ごこくじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島市]]草牟田にある[[別表神社]]。正式名「'''鹿児島県護國神社'''」。
== 沿革 == [[戊辰戦争]]で戦死した兵士を祭る「''靖献霊社''」に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に作られていた。[[1941年]]（[[昭和16年]]）に現在地に移転、神社名を現名称に変更する。
[[Category:鹿児島県の神社|ここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|かこしまここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|かこしまここくしんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島県護国神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture defense of the fatherland Shinto shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市草牟田二丁目60-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 鹿児島県出身の英霊 |社格 = 内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建 = 明治元年（1868年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]・[[10月13日]] |神事 = }}
'''鹿児島県護国神社'''（かごしまけんごごくじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[草牟田|草牟田二丁目]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。
== 祭神 == [[明治維新]]以降の国事に殉じた鹿児島県出身の[[英霊]]や、殉職した[[自衛官]]、[[警察官]]、[[消防士]]等77,000余柱を祀る。
== 歴史 == [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）、[[明治天皇]]から与えられた[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]の戦死者を祀るための金500両により創建された'''靖献霊社'''（いさたまれいしゃ）に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に鎮座していた。明治8年に'''鹿児島招魂社'''に改称、[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）に鹿児島県護国神社に改称した。[[戦後]]の昭和22年（[[1947年]]）、軍国主義施設として廃止されるのを免れるため、'''薩隅頌徳神社'''（さつぐうしょうとくじんじゃ）に改称し、翌23年に現在地に[[遷座]]、同26年に現社名に復した。
== 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、弓道場（兼部室）が当校に出来るまでは、弓道場を使用させてもらっていた。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式）
{{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島県護国神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture defense of the fatherland Shinto shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市草牟田二丁目60-7 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 鹿児島県出身の英霊 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建 = 明治元年（1868年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]・[[10月13日]] |神事 = }}

== 歴史 == [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）、[[明治天皇]]から与えられた[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]の戦死者を祀るための金500両により創建された'''靖献霊社'''（いさたまれいしゃ）に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に鎮座していた。明治8年に'''鹿児島招魂社'''に改称、[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）に鹿児島県護国神社に改称した。[[戦後]]の昭和22年（[[1947年]]）、軍国主義施設として廃止されるのを免れるため、'''薩隅頌徳神社'''（さつぐうしょうとくじんじゃ）に改称し、翌23年に現在地に[[遷座]]、同26年に現社名に復した。
== 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、弓道場（兼部室）が当校に出来るまでは、弓道場を使用させてもらっていた。

平成18年2月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:鹿児島市]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

平成19年4月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

平成19年4月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kagoshima Prefecture defense of the fatherland Shinto shrine.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. revision.comment: 「Kagoshima_Prefecture_defense_of_the_fatherland_Shinto_shrine.jpg」 を 「Kagoshima_Prefecture_Gokoku_Shinto_shrine.jpg」 に差し替え（[[commons:User:CommonsDelinker|CommonsDelinker]]による。理由：[[:c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[:c:COM:FR#FR2|Criterion 2]] (meaningless or amb
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:09 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島県護国神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市草牟田二丁目60番7号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鹿児島県護国神社}} |祭神 = 鹿児島県出身の国事殉難者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建 = 明治元年（1868年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]・[[10月13日]] |神事 = }} '''鹿児島県護国神社'''（かごしまけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[草牟田|草牟田二丁目]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 == 祭神 == [[明治維新]]以降の国事に殉じた鹿児島県出身の殉死者や、殉職した[[自衛官]]、[[警察官]]、[[消防士]]等77,000柱余を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）、[[明治天皇]]から与えられた[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]の戦死者を祀るための金500両により創建された'''靖献霊社'''（いさたまれいしゃ）に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に鎮座していた。明治8年に'''鹿児島招魂社'''に改称、[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）に鹿児島県護国神社に改称した。[[戦後]]の昭和22年（[[1947年]]）、軍国主義施設として廃止されるのを免れるため、'''薩隅頌徳神社'''（さつぐうしょうとくじんじゃ）に改称し、翌23年に現在地に[[遷座]]、同26年に現社名に復した。
平成19年4月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg | map_type = Japan | caption = Torii[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] [[Category:Wikipedifa drafts]] [[Category:Wikipedisa drafts]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==Japanese content== {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == Deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:Original pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == Deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == Deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[category:original pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==Japanese content== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[category:original pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kagoshima Prefecture defense of the fatherland Shinto shrine.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==Japanese content== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==Japanese content== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]

It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==Japanese content== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Jinja (Kagoshima)|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{Shinto Navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[Category:dd]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Images]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} {{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines {{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines {{draft}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = Fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社[[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} '''Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja''' (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}}
