revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also! == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also! == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Be11yo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Be11yo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/be11you/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft}} is located o11osite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} {{citation needed|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}}{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural tier 2]] [[Category:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja}} {{citation needed|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}}{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1868 establishments]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pp\]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}}
