[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|12=draft}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine|12=draft}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=draft}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:09 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島県護国神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = [[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]]草牟田二丁目60番7号 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鹿児島県護国神社}} |祭神 = 鹿児島県出身の国事殉難者 |社格 = 旧内務大臣指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月13日]]・[[10月13日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=鹿児島県
護国神社|coordinates= {{coord2|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E}}}} }} '''鹿児島県護国神社'''（かごしまけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[草牟田|草牟田二丁目]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 == 祭神 == [[明治維新]]以降の国事に殉じた鹿児島県出身の殉死者や、殉職した[[自衛官]]、[[警察官]]、[[消防士]]等77,000柱余を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）、[[明治天皇]]から与えられた[[鳥羽・伏見の戦い]]の戦死者を祀るための金500両により創建された'''靖献霊社'''（いさたまれいしゃ）に始まる。当初は[[照国神社]]の右手横（現在の戊辰駐車場）に鎮座していた。明治8年に'''鹿児島招魂社'''に改称、[[昭和]]14年（[[1939年]]）に鹿児島県護国神社に改称した。[[戦後]]の昭和22年（[[1947年]]）、軍国主義施設として廃止されるのを免れるため、'''薩隅頌徳神社'''（さつぐうしょうとくじんじゃ）に改称し、翌23年に現在地に[[遷座]]、同26年に現社名に復した。
平成19年4月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

平成19年4月には[[特攻勇士の像]]が建立された。 == 神社周辺 == * [[鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] - 当神社の向かいにあり、学校の弓道場（兼部室）が完成するまでは、護国神社の弓道場を使用していた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ 鹿児島縣護國神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしまここくしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima (city)|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|ja|4月13日}} and {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima City|ja|鹿児島市|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|ja|鳥羽・伏見の戦い|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni Shrine|ja|照国神社|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]]

It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676849]] Wikidata name: Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}}

They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine.
