Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}}

Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}}

After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11676849]] Wikidata name: Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, pp February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] and [[:en:October 13|October 13]] }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the [[:en:Battle of Toba–Fushimi|Toba-Fushimi War]], the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of [[:en:Terukuni shrine|Terukuni Shrine]] (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|WD=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} and {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|WD=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} and {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|WD=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} and {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13|1=October 13}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|WD=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine|1=Terukuni shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} and {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13|1=October 13}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|WD=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine|1=Terukuni shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.k-gokoku.or.jp/ Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja Official Website] * == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|version=102022421|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Commons category|Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11676849}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kagoshimaken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] [[ja:鹿児島県護国神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja (Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine) | image = [[File:Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and Haiden | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagoshima | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|32.7|N|130|32|30.1|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kusumuda 2-chōme 60-7, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|qid=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} | deity = 77,000 fallen heroes of Kagoshima Prefecture who died in service to the nation | type = Former Home Ministry-designated Gokoku Jinja, {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | founded = Meiji 1 (1868) | festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|qid=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} and {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|qid=Q2925|lt=October 13|1=October 13}} }} {{ill|Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (鹿児島県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in Kusumuda 2-chōme, {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|qid=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == deity Enshrined == The shrine enshrines over 77,000 spirits from Kagoshima Prefecture who perished in service to the nation since the [[Meiji Restoration]]: including those who died in national crises, Self-Defense Forces personnel who died in the line of duty, as well as police officers and firefighters who lost their lives serving the public. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), utilizing 500 ryō of gold granted by Emperor Meiji to honor those who fell in the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|qid=Q1193959|lt=Toba-Fushimi War|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}}, the shrine was first established as the '''Isetamareisha (靖献霊社)'''. Initially, it stood to the right side of {{ill|Terukuni shrine|en|Terukuni shrine|qid=Q704219|lt=Terukuni Shrine|1=Terukuni shrine}} (currently, near the Boshin parking area). In Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed "Kagoshima Shōkonsha," and in Shōwa 14 (1939), it took the name "Kagoshima-ken Gokoku Jinja." After World War II, in Shōwa 22 (1947), to avoid being abolished as a militaristic facility, it was temporarily renamed "Satsugū Shōtoku Jinja" (薩隅頌徳神社). The following year (Shōwa 23), it was relocated to its current location, and in Shōwa 26 (1951), it reverted to its current name. In April Heisei 19 (2007), a statue commemorating Special Attack Force heroes (Tokkō Yūshi) was erected on the grounds. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Kagoshima Technical High School|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Technical High School|qq=}} is located opposite the shrine. Before the school’s own archery range (dōjō) was completed, its archery club used the Gokoku Shrine’s archery range. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|qid=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|qid=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qid=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qid=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|qid=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qid=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|qid=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|qid=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|qid=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|qid=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|qid=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} ==
